title: foeniculum officinale subsp. sativum
intro: Foeniculum officinale subsp. sativum, commonly known as common or garden sweet fennel, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is widely cultivated for its edible seeds and leaves. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Foeniculum officinale subsp. sativum include reducing flatulence and bloating, relieving pain and discomfort, and aiding in the treatment of various respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
